figures.caption: Figure 2: Structural alignment between SASRec item embeddings (◦) and low-rank transition statistics (△) across three datasets. Each panel shows the item–item cosine similarity matrix within a single embedding space, where brighter colors indicate more similar items.

Figure 4: Architecture of the encoder (Section 4.3.1) step 𝑡 . The encoder takes five low-rank embeddings as input: the user collaborative embedding ucf (𝑢), the collaborative embeddings of the previous and current items icf (𝑖𝑢,𝑡−1) and icf (𝑖𝑢,𝑡 ), and the source- and destination-side transition embeddings isrc (𝑖𝑢,𝑡−1) and idst (𝑖𝑢,𝑡 ). ⊞ denotes feature concatenation.

Table 1: Main results on five benchmarks. We report top-𝑘 ranking metrics for standard trained-on-target sequential recommenders and training-free baselines, along with SRPFN (evaluated with |𝐷support | = 16). The best and second-best results in each row are denoted in bold and underlined, respectively. †: baselines trained on the target domain training split. ‡: training-free baselines computed from the target domain training split without gradient updates.
Table 9: Full-catalog ranking results across five datasets. The best and second-best results in each row are denoted in bold and underlined, respectively.
